# scroll_100k QA artifacts

Outputs of [`scripts/qa_viz.py`](../../../../scripts/qa_viz.py) over the
`scroll_100k` headline run. Refreshed by hand after each cost-sweep
re-run; not auto-generated by `make scroll-100k`.

The script's job is narrow: catch the things a single eyeballing of
`report.json` misses — cost-field arithmetic that doesn't add up,
degenerate "win" cells where a policy prunes essentially everything,
and apples-to-oranges comparisons across different RAM budgets.

## How to regenerate

```bash
# point at the sweep dir (recommended — captures every (policy, ℓ) cell)
make qa-viz REPORT=testdata/runs/scroll_100k/sweep_v2

# or at a single bundled report
make qa-viz REPORT=testdata/runs/scroll_100k/report.json
```

Both write into `testdata/runs/scroll_100k/qa/` by default.
Override with `QA_OUT=<dir>` (e.g. `qa_v3` for a parallel sweep).
Output is deterministic — two runs against the same input produce
byte-identical files except for the `out_dir` field in
`qa_summary.json`.

## Files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `qa_summary.json` | Top-level summary: cell count, policies, ℓ values, cost-consistency check, degenerate-cell count, same-RAM-band comparison. |
| `degeneracy_flags.json` | One entry per flagged cell, each with the three numeric reasons that tripped. Empty list = no degenerate cells in the sweep. |
| `cost_decomposition.svg` | Stacked `RAMCost + MissPenaltyAgg = TotalCost` bars per cell. Hand-written SVG, no matplotlib. |
| `pareto_ramratio_coverage.svg` | Scatter of `RAMRatio` (x) vs `HotHitCoverage` (y), one dot per cell, coloured by policy. |

## QA logic

### 1. Cost arithmetic

For every cell, verify `TotalCost == RAMCost + MissPenaltyAgg` within
`rel_tol = 1e-9`. A failure usually means the field schema drifted
(rename / unit change) and downstream comparisons are silently wrong.

### 2. Degenerate-cell flag

A cell is flagged only when **all three** of these are true (so a single
extreme metric isn't enough to trip the flag):

| Metric | Default threshold | Meaning when tripped |
|---|---|---|
| `RAMRatio` | `< 0.001` | Policy keeps < 0.1% of slots hot — "demote almost everything" |
| `SlotBlocksPruned / TotalExposure` | `> 0.99` | > 99% of slot-block exposure was pruned |
| `HotHitCoverage` | `< 0.30` | < 30% of accesses landed on a hot slot |

The thresholds are tunable via `--ramratio-degen`, `--pruned-frac-degen`,
`--hot-hit-degen`. The defaults are chosen so a "policy minimises
`TotalCost` by demoting everything and just paying the miss penalty"
regime is caught, but a genuinely sparse hot set (small but not
pathological) is not.

### 3. Same-RAM-band comparison

Cost comparisons across cells with different `RAMRatio` are
apples-to-oranges — a smaller hot set always has lower `RAMCost`
just by definition. The script picks two policies (default
`fixed-1k:statistical`) and only compares cells whose `RAMRatio`
falls in `[0.060, 0.062]`. Tunable via `--same-ram-lo` /
`--same-ram-hi` / `--compare A:B`.

## Current results (sweep_v2 — 4 policies × 10 ℓ values, 40 cells)

- **Cost arithmetic**: 0 of 40 cells failed the consistency check.
- **Degenerate cells**: 1 — `statistical` @ ℓ=32. RAMRatio 0.04%,
  pruned_frac 99.96%, HotHitCoverage 18.8%. At this cost ratio the
  `c·τ/ℓ` threshold drops low enough that the policy demotes almost
  every slot, then takes the miss penalty hit — a "win" on TotalCost
  that has no predictive content. Do not cite ℓ=32 numbers as a model
  result.
- **Same-RAM band [0.060, 0.062]**: only one (policy, ℓ) pair from
  `fixed-1k` and `statistical` falls in band — both at ℓ=512:

  | | fixed-1k | statistical | Δ (fixed-1k − stat) |
  |---|---:|---:|---:|
  | RAMRatio | 0.06156 | 0.06130 | +0.00026 |
  | HotHitCoverage | 0.9209 | 0.8157 | **+0.1051** |
  | FalsePruneRate | 0.0791 | 0.1843 | **−0.1051** |
  | TotalCost | 32,884,229 | 40,549,660 | −7,665,431 |

  At the *same* RAM budget, the fixed-1k baseline beats the statistical
  policy on coverage by ~10.5 pp on this single comparable cell.
  This is one ℓ value at one RAM band, not a sweep-wide claim — see
  [.local/scroll_100k_notes.md](../../../../.local/scroll_100k_notes.md)
  for the full caveat list.

## When to refresh

Re-run after any of:
- A new cost-sweep over different ℓ values lands in `sweep_*/`.
- Field names in `simulate_*.json` change (the cost-arithmetic check
  catches schema drift, but only after you re-run).
- The QA thresholds are tuned (in which case commit the new
  `qa_summary.json` so the next reader sees the new defaults).
